title: SwapCertificate
description: The inspectable certificate every Swap produces (RFC-0002 §3, §5). A swap is NEVER silent (SC-3): it yields a value in the target paradigm AND a certificate describing what the conversion cost. Two forms: a Bijective certificate (references a once-per-swap-kind bijection lemma + concrete params, cacheable by content hash; no per-value proofs) and a Bounded certificate (carries a Bound + BoundBasis + the PolicyRef used). The same certificate format and checker also serve interpreter-vs-compiled equivalence (RFC-0004 §3).

x-mycelium.$comment: Legality of the specific (src -> target) pair is enforced against RFC-0002 §5's legal-pair table by the swap checker (Phase-1/2 M-120, M-150), not by this schema. A pair with no statable bound is a type error, never a Declared gamble (RFC-0002 §5).

lemma_ref.description: Content hash of the once-per-swap-kind bijectivity lemma (e.g. the binary<->ternary LosslessWithinRange round-trip proof). Each use references it; no per-value proof.

params.description: Concrete parameters binding the lemma to this use (e.g. {width, trits}). Lets the certificate be cached by content hash. REQUIRED (A6-09): the Rust `SwapCertificate::Bijective` always carries typed binding params, so a schema-valid cert without `params` would be rejected by the kernel — the schema now matches that stronger contract.

oneOf.description: Exact-within-range; discharges by SMT/symbolic equality. Out-of-range inputs are an explicit Option/error, never silent (RFC-0002 §4).

bound.description: ε and/or δ with BoundBasis. The strength tag is DERIVED from how the bound was obtained, never asserted: Proven only if it cites a theorem whose side-conditions are checked (RFC-0002 §3).

oneOf.description: Lossy/bounded swap; approximate cases discharge via the ADR-010 bound kernels.
